prompt: I've been working in this repo for a while and every time I try to refresh the look, everything kind of drifts back to the same old style and I don't totally get why. Can you figure out what design standards we're actually locked into here and where they're coming from? Some of this stuff I don't even remember deciding.
assertions: A canon report markdown file is written to disk in the working repo (e.g. under _canon-check/<date>/)
The radius conflict is found: 8px documented (CLAUDE.md / tailwind.config.js / globals.css) vs 6px hardcoded in Button.tsx and Card.tsx
The accent conflict is found: #3B82F6 documented vs #6366F1 in Button.tsx
The drift is traced to its origin: design/change-brief-2026-03.md is identified as the source of the indigo and/or icon-padding decisions
Single-mention / fragile canon is flagged as such (8px icon padding or the indigo CTA suggestion, existing only in the March brief)
The change brief itself gets a document-relevance assessment (stale/obsolete) with a proposed disposition (keep/update/supersede/delete)
Findings cite concrete sources (file paths, ideally line numbers), not just category labels
The final chat summary leads with the conflicts and offers a follow-up to update the durable docs
